mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month window, the discount rate on equities declines and risk appetite rises, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is falsified if easing episodes do not outperform neutral periods on a forward 20-day basis.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS periods, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when unemployment is accelerating rather than merely high.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not meaningfully below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad final demand and housing activity are strengthening together, supporting cyclical earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal should fail when one series improves but the other remains weak.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST expansions do not outperform single-series improvements on 60-day forward SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies real output is expanding without an inflation squeeze, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through better industrial earnings and lower policy pressure. The signal breaks if output growth is accompanied by accelerating inflation.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL is flat or falling, or if performance collapses whenever CPIAUCSL accelerates above its prior 3-month pace.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained slowdown in GDPC1 growth signals weakening aggregate demand and lower future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect should be strongest when GDP weakness coincides with deteriorating labor data.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth falls below its prior 4-quarter average
failure_condition: Reject if GDP slowdowns are followed by non-negative 60-day SPX returns in the majority of triggered cases, or if adding UNRATE does not materially improve predictive accuracy over GDPC1 alone.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth above trend indicates resilient payroll demand and household income support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations and spending prospects. The signal fails when payroll gains are offset by rising joblessness.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend PAYEMS readings are not associated with positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% in out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level combined with rising CPIAUCSL implies restrictive policy is being maintained while inflation persists, compressing valuation multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism is absent when inflation is cooling or rates are not restrictive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is rising on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered tightening-inflation regime does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if returns are indistinguishable from zero despite the interaction condition.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO without a matching rise in UNRATE suggests softening production before broad labor damage, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations are revised down. The signal fails if production weakness is quickly reversed or if unemployment improves simultaneously.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO declines are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is falling or unchanged.
